ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'hot pot food product' class (FOODON_03315595); no specific mala steamboat set entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated mala steamboat code. A0CKP (mixed cooked dish) used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for mala steamboat set as a distinct food concept.

main_ingredients: mala broth (tallow/lard base, dried chili, Sichuan peppercorn, spice paste)

serving.natural_language_portion: One individual mala steamboat set typically weighs approximately 500 g and includes broth, assorted proteins (beef slices, fish balls, tofu), and vegetables served in a personal hot pot.

nutrient_source.title: Steamboat (Mala) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F08010058 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95. All per_100g macronutrient values are preserved from the HPB source. Saturated fat is estimated from the known tallow/lard-based mala broth composition as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; estimated at ~5.5 g/100g based on the fat fraction typical of tallow-dominant broths (USDA FoodData Central, beef tallow reference NDB 04626).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mala steamboat set. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed hot pot / broth-based dish with moderate carbohydrate from glass noodles and vegetables. The carbohydrate fraction (6.15 g/100g) is primarily from vegetables and optional noodles; the high fat content (12.81 g/100g) from tallow-based broth further attenuates glycemic response. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (45 × 6.15 × 5) / 100 = 13.8. Analogous reference: mixed Asian broth-based dish, University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 434 mg/100g; cumulative intake from broth and dipping sauces substantially exceeds safe limits per serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid drinking the mala broth directly, omit all dipping sauces, and strictly limit portion size to minimise sodium intake, as a 500 g serving delivers approximately 2170 mg sodium — nearly the full daily recommended limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (6.15 g/100g) and low sugar (1.01 g/100g) are favourable, but high fat (12.81 g/100g) from tallow-based broth may impair insulin sensitivity and slow gastric emptying unpredictably
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume mala steamboat in moderate portions without noodles or rice, choosing lean proteins and vegetables, while avoiding sweet dipping sauces and monitoring total fat intake from the tallow-based broth.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~5.5 g/100g from tallow/lard-based broth, combined with high sodium 434 mg/100g, presents a dual cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit mala steamboat consumption due to high saturated fat from tallow-based broth and high sodium content; if consumed, choose lean proteins, avoid drinking the broth, and restrict portion to no more than 200 g.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~5.5 g/100g from tallow/lard, sodium 434 mg/100g, pro-inflammatory chili and Sichuan peppercorn, and arachidonic acid from beef slices
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid mala steamboat, particularly during flares, as the tallow-based broth, nightshade spices, and red meat collectively amplify inflammatory pathways and worsen joint swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mala Steamboat Set (1 Pax) is an individual-portion Singaporean hot pot dish featuring a spicy, numbing Sichuan-style mala broth as its base. The broth is made from tallow or lard rendered with dried chili, Sichuan peppercorn, doubanjiang (fermented chili bean paste), garlic, ginger, and aromatic spices, producing a deeply red, intensely flavoured cooking liquid. Diners cook assorted raw ingredients — typically beef slices, fish balls, tofu, leafy greens, mushrooms, and optionally glass noodles — directly in the simmering broth at the table. The dish is characterised by its bold 'má là' (numbing-spicy) flavour profile, rich oily mouthfeel, and interactive dining format. Nutritionally, the set delivers 150 kcal/100g with a high fat content of 12.81 g/100g (predominantly saturated fat from tallow, estimated ~5.5 g/100g), moderate protein at 2.87 g/100g, and relatively low carbohydrate at 6.15 g/100g. Sodium is elevated at 434 mg/100g, making it a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The high saturated fat and pro-inflammatory spice components make it unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. For Type 2 Diabetes, the low carbohydrate profile is favourable when noodles are omitted, though the high fat content warrants monitoring. Mala steamboat is popular in Singapore's Chinese-Singaporean dining scene and is widely available at dedicated mala hotpot restaurants and food courts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in mala hotpot?
Is mala steamboat high in sodium?
What is in a mala steamboat set?
Is mala hotpot suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central beef tallow reference and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, appropriately flagged.
notes: Saturated fat (5.5 g/100g) is estimated from the known tallow/lard-dominant mala broth composition using USDA FoodData Central beef tallow reference (NDB 04626); HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this composite mala steamboat set are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Input cultural tags (breakfast, soft_foods, wholegrain, low_calorie) were reviewed and corrected: mala steamboat is not a breakfast food, not a wholegrain food, and is not low-calorie at 150 kcal/100g with high fat; these tags have been removed. Occasion tag corrected to dinner/supper/social dining. Halal status is marked false as tallow/lard-based mala broth typically contains pork-derived lard; consumers should verify with individual restaurants.
